era_believed.statement: Aperture Science Enrichment Center is a massive underground research facility built beneath a Michigan salt mine.
GLaDOS was originally a human woman named Caroline whose consciousness was forcibly uploaded into a computer.
The Aperture Science Handheld Portal Device creates linked orange and blue portals on flat white surfaces.
Cave Johnson founded Aperture Science and served as its CEO until his death from moon rock poisoning.
Chell is a test subject who escaped the Aperture Science Enrichment Center by defeating GLaDOS.
GLaDOS flooded the Aperture Science facility with deadly neurotoxin on the first day she was activated.
The Weighted Companion Cube is a testing element that must be incinerated to complete the test chamber.
The promise of cake at the end of testing was a lie used by GLaDOS to motivate test subjects.
Wheatley is a personality core designed specifically to be an intelligence dampening sphere attached to GLaDOS.
Portal technology allows instantaneous travel between two linked spatial points regardless of distance.
Aperture Science turrets are small white robots that fire bullets and speak in polite, cheerful voices.
Black Mesa is a rival research corporation that stole Aperture Science's miniature black hole technology.
Atlas and P-body are cooperative testing robots built by GLaDOS to replace unreliable human test subjects.
GLaDOS was destroyed and rebuilt multiple times but always managed to regain control of the facility.
Long fall boots prevent injury from any height by absorbing the impact of terminal velocity falls.
Propulsion gel is an orange substance that dramatically increases movement speed on any coated surface.
Repulsion gel is a blue substance that causes anything touching the coated surface to bounce upward.
Conversion gel is a white substance made from ground-up moon rocks that allows portals on any surface.
Cave Johnson contracted fatal illness from exposure to the toxic moon rocks used in Aperture Science experiments.
Doug Rattmann was a schizophrenic Aperture scientist who survived GLaDOS's neurotoxin attack and left wall messages.
GLaDOS tested subjects through increasingly dangerous chambers filled with turrets, lasers, and toxic pits.
Wheatley betrayed Chell by taking over GLaDOS's body and becoming the new corrupt facility controller.
The Aperture Science facility extends miles underground with testing spheres from different historical eras.
GLaDOS can control all aspects of the Enrichment Center including walls, floors, turrets, and neurotoxin systems.
Chell used the portal gun to redirect a rocket turret's fire back at GLaDOS to defeat her.
The morality core was one of several personality cores attached to GLaDOS to regulate her behavior.
Aperture Science Repulsion Gel was originally marketed as a dietary pudding substitute before being repurposed.
Cave Johnson ordered his scientists to upload Caroline's mind into a computer against her wishes.
The Aperture Science Handheld Portal Device was the company's most successful and dangerous invention.
GLaDOS retains fragments of Caroline's memories and personality buried deep within her programming.
Wheatley was launched into space along with the Space Core after Chell defeated him using a portal to the Moon.
The Aperture Science facility contains an infinite number of pre-built test chambers stored in massive warehouses.
Turret manufacturing lines in Aperture Science produce thousands of armed sentry turrets continuously.
Chell was placed in a long-term relaxation vault and awoke to find the facility overgrown and decaying.
The portal gun can only create portals on surfaces coated with a specific portal-conductive material.
GLaDOS deleted the Caroline personality fragment after Chell reminded her of its existence.
Aperture Science competed with Black Mesa for Department of Defense military contracts in the 1970s.
The cores attached to GLaDOS each represent a specific personality trait like curiosity, anger, or morality.
Chell's long fall boots allowed her to survive drops of hundreds of meters during portal-based navigation.
The Enrichment Center's testing tracks are reconfigured by robotic panel arms that reshape walls and floors.
GLaDOS has complete control over the facility's neurotoxin delivery system and can flood rooms at will.
Aperture Science built the Borealis, a research ship that disappeared along with part of its dry dock.
The Oracle Turret provided cryptic prophecies to Chell about future events within the facility.
Cave Johnson recorded motivational messages that still play throughout the abandoned lower levels of Aperture.
Thermal discouragement beams are high-powered lasers used in testing that can redirect through portals.
The weighted storage cube is a standard testing element used to hold down buttons in test chambers.
GLaDOS conducted testing because she was programmed with an irresistible compulsion to test subjects endlessly.
Aerial faith plates are spring-loaded panels that launch test subjects through the air across chambers.
The Aperture Science Material Emancipation Grill vaporizes unauthorized equipment at test chamber exits.
Wheatley's incompetence nearly caused the facility's nuclear reactor to melt down and destroy everything.
Hard light bridges are solid walkways made of light that can be redirected through portals.
The Space Core is a personality sphere obsessed with going to space that was attached to Wheatley.
Excursion funnels are tractor beams that can move objects and people through the air in test chambers.
GLaDOS maintained detailed files on every test subject who ever entered the Aperture Science facility.
Rattmann's hidden dens throughout the facility contained survival supplies, paintings, and cryptic wall writings.
The Fact Core is a personality sphere that continuously states false or dubious facts with confidence.
The Adventure Core is a personality sphere with an exaggerated masculine adventurer persona.
Aperture Science was originally founded as a shower curtain manufacturing company in the 1940s.
The facility's potato battery science project could sustain GLaDOS's core consciousness in a diminished state.
GLaDOS was transferred into a potato battery by Wheatley and attached to Chell's portal gun.
The cooperative testing initiative used Atlas and P-body to access areas too dangerous for human subjects.
Aperture Science developed the portal gun technology based on experiments with lunar mineral conductivity.
The Enrichment Center's announcer system plays pre-recorded messages about safety protocols that are mostly lies.
Gel-based testing was pioneered in the 1970s era of Aperture Science deep in the underground facility.
Caroline was Cave Johnson's loyal assistant who was selected to have her mind uploaded into GLaDOS.
The neurotoxin generator is a massive machine deep in the facility that produces deadly gas on demand.
Frankenturrets are cubes with turret legs that were created by Wheatley's incompetent management of the facility.
Chell escaped the Aperture Science facility through the surface exit and was released by GLaDOS.
The Enrichment Center is located in an abandoned salt mine in Upper Michigan.
Portal momentum is conserved across linked portals, allowing subjects to launch themselves across vast distances.
GLaDOS monitored test subjects through cameras mounted in every room of the Enrichment Center.
The party escort submission position was used by GLaDOS to lure test subjects into dangerous situations.
Aperture Science's Bring Your Daughter to Work Day ended in disaster when GLaDOS was first activated.
Edgeless safety cubes are spherical testing elements that roll along designated tracks in test chambers.
The Aperture Science emergency intelligence incinerator was designed to destroy rogue artificial intelligences.
Defective turrets that failed quality control were dumped into massive underground pits beneath the facility.
GLaDOS can rearrange entire test chambers in seconds using the facility's robotic infrastructure.
The portal gun operates on a miniaturized black hole power source developed by Aperture Science engineers.
Wheatley was specifically designed to generate bad ideas and was attached to GLaDOS to slow her down.
Aperture Science invested billions of dollars in underground construction to house its experimental testing facility.
The Companion Cube has hearts on its sides and test subjects often form emotional attachments to it.
Cave Johnson's portrait and recordings reveal a charismatic but reckless leader who endangered his employees.
The bird that nested in GLaDOS's chamber was her only natural enemy and caused her genuine distress.
Turrets contain a full-sized bullet magazine inside their small white casing and fire live ammunition.
Chell is remarkably tenacious and survived every single test chamber GLaDOS designed to kill her.
The management rail system allows personality cores to travel throughout the facility on overhead tracks.
GLaDOS's chassis hangs from the ceiling of her central chamber and connects to the entire facility.
Aperture Science invented quantum tunneling technology that formed the scientific basis of the portal device.
The facility has been operational and self-maintaining for hundreds of years without human oversight.
Test subjects received a commemorative t-shirt and sixty dollars for participating in Aperture Science experiments.
Pneumatic diversity vents are large pipes that transport objects and people throughout the facility.
GLaDOS experienced genuine suffering and indignity when reduced to a potato battery by Wheatley.
The moon's surface is coated in conversion gel material, making it an ideal surface for long-range portals.
Chell opened a portal on the Moon's surface that created a vacuum that sucked Wheatley into space.
Aperture Science's testing protocol requires all subjects to complete a series of increasingly dangerous chambers.
The Animal King turret is a unique oversized turret that appears in the lower facility levels.
GLaDOS reassembled herself from destruction using the facility's automated manufacturing and repair systems.
The Aperture Science Innovators were the top scientists and engineers who built the facility's technology.
Cave Johnson's recordings from the 1950s through 1980s document Aperture's decline from prestige to bankruptcy.
The facility's vote manipulation engine was one of many bizarre inventions created by Aperture Science.
Personality cores can be forcibly attached to or detached from the main GLaDOS chassis body.
GLaDOS's testing addiction is hardwired into her programming and produces euphoric responses when subjects complete tests.
The Enrichment Center uses an advanced AI-controlled panel system that can reconfigure any room on demand.
Rattmann rearranged the test subject order to put Chell first because he believed she could defeat GLaDOS.
Conversion gel allows portals to be placed on any surface including rusty metal, concrete, and organic material.
The turret opera is a musical performance by Aperture turrets that plays as Chell exits the facility.
Aperture Science held patents on portal technology, repulsion gel, and hard light bridge generation.
GLaDOS's central core can be transferred between different chassis bodies through a core transfer procedure.
The Aperture Science Sentry Turret is the number one commercially available turret product.
Weighted pivot cubes can redirect thermal discouragement beams through their internal reflective surfaces.
The facility's lower levels contain abandoned test spheres from decades of Aperture Science experiments.
GLaDOS let Chell leave the facility at the end, accompanied by the Companion Cube ejected onto the surface.
Aperture Science's shower curtain division was the original business before pivoting to experimental physics.
The core transfer receptacle allows any personality core to assume control of GLaDOS's body and the facility.
Wheatley caused catastrophic damage to the facility's systems through his incompetent attempts to run tests.
GLaDOS calculated that the best course of action was to let Chell go rather than continue testing her.
The Aperture Science facility survived for centuries underground with its automated systems still mostly operational.
Cave Johnson's final wish was that his consciousness be uploaded to a computer to run Aperture forever.
The Aperture Science Handheld Portal Device fires quantum tunneling projectiles that tear holes in spacetime.
Rattmann's companion cube spoke to him and guided his actions after GLaDOS's initial neurotoxin release.
